Thus, graft placement fails predictably in Class III compared to Classes I and II, making it the correct answer based on available evidence.

(Note: Final answer refers to Class III.)

The key clinical finding is that connective tissue grafts provide no predictable root coverage in Class III gingival recurrences because of inherent structural limitations. As a result, graft placement consistently fails in this category, distinguishing it as having the worst prognosis among all classes.  

Final Answer: \boxed{C}

(Answer refers to Class III.) Based solely on the provided information:

Class III gingival recession has no predictable root coverage after connective tissue grafting due to its severe anatomy and inability for proper healing; therefore, graft placement reliably fails here while being more effective in milder forms like Class I and II.

Final Answer:\boxed{C}. Answer choice: C
